title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: blockr.stem R package extends blockr low-code framework (blockr.core + blockr.ggplot) STEM-branded blocks data pipeline: import data set → select/plot categorical (survey) variable stemtools → apply Stem house theme → export PNG/SVG native, editable PowerPoint chart. output oriented around stemtools::stem_barplot() / stem_inline() survey charts.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: standard R package roxygen2 docs testthat (edition 3). build system beyond R/devtools; run R session repo root (e.g. R -q -e '<expr>'): Run tests: devtools::test() Run one test file: devtools::test(filter = "expr-builders") (matches tests/testthat/test-expr-builders.R; filter name test-) Regenerate docs + NAMESPACE changing roxygen @ tags exports: devtools::document() — NAMESPACE man/*.Rd generated; never hand-edit . Full package check: devtools::check() Load interactive work: devtools::load_all() MCP server (r-btw / r-mcptools) available driving live R session dedicated tools (btw_tool_pkg_test, btw_tool_pkg_document, btw_tool_pkg_check, btw_tool_pkg_load_all) — prefer R session already running.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R/zzz.R’s .onLoad() calls blockr.core::register_blocks() exported new_*_block() constructor, assigning category (input / transform / plot / output), icon description. Adding block means adding constructor register_blocks() entry .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Every block new_*_block() function wraps blockr.core/blockr.ggplot constructor (new_file_block, new_transform_block, new_ggplot_transform_block) passes three things: expr — reactive() builds unevaluated language object (see ), state — named list reactiveVals, one per constructor argument. UI function function(id) returning block’s controls (Shiny inputs, tags$details disclosures advanced options). class, allow_empty_state, usually expr_type = "bquoted". allow_empty_state lists every state field may legitimately read empty (optional columns, NA-able sizes, “auto” toggles). Omitting field makes block gate “waiting inputs set” never render — common cause block silently nothing.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Blocks compute results directly; emit code blockr evaluates. helpers expr-builders.R construct calls bquote/.call, using .(data) marker placeholder blockr binds upstream data (hence expr_type = "bquoted"). Key builders: - stem_plot_expr() — stem_barplot() / stem_inline() call, optionally wrapped blockr.stem::set_label_size(). Shared Chart Visualize blocks. - stem_theme_expr() — appends stemtools::theme_stem() + margin/font layers base plot. Shared Theme Visualize blocks. - stem_select_expr() — subsets chosen column(s). - stem_read_expr() — dispatches picked file path readr (delimited), readxl (spreadsheets) rio::import() (everything else).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Variable Selector block tags output data stem_weight / stem_group attributes (via structure() stem_select_expr()). Downstream plot blocks read stem_effective_weight() / stem_effective_group(), weight/grouping chosen upstream overrides plot block’s control. touching weighting grouping, keep Selector (authoritative) plot block’s fallback sync.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: STEM Import (stem_import_block.R) — root data block. Exists blockr.core’s filebrowser_block outputs bare path, read call; wraps shinyFiles-picked path real reader. Ships self-contained gear-popover (inline CSS/JS) format options. STEM Variable Selector (stem_var_selector_block.R) — searchable DT table categorical vars; row click outputs single column. Custom block_output shows stemtools::stem_summarise_cat() frequency table. STEM Chart (stem_chart_block.R) STEM Visualize (stem_visualize_block.R) — Chart plots ; Visualize Chart + Theme fused one block (shares stem_plot_expr/stem_theme_expr). Theme STEM (theme_stem_block.R) — applies theme_stem() upstream ggplot. STEM Export (stem_export_block.R, pptx-chart.R) — preview + download. live preview uses ggsave() call download see matches file; preview reads controls NS("expr", <name>) blockr runs block server nested "expr" namespace. pptx-chart.R reconstructs tidy category/value/series frame ggplot’s $data/$mapping emit native, editable mschart Office chart (works reconstructable STEM plots; errors otherwise).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Comments codebase unusually explanatory explain (blockr quirks, ggplot internals, mschart/officer sizing). Match reason non-obvious; don’t add noise isn’t. %||% (null-coalescing) defined locally R/stem_read_expr.R. Optional heavy deps Suggests guarded call site requireNamespace() (mschart/officer pptx, svglite SVG).

code: @Manual{,   title = {blockr.stem: Stem Theme Block for blockr},   author = {Aleš Vomáčka},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.3},   url = {https://stem-cz.github.io/blockr.stem/}, }

title: Stem Theme Block for blockr
text: blockr extension provides STEM-branded blocks full survey-charting pipeline.

title: Stem Theme Block for blockr
text: Install using:

title: STEM Chart block — new_stem_chart_block
text: blockr transform block plots single variable upstream data frame using one stemtools plotting functions. user picks variable chooses bar plot (stemtools::stem_barplot()) inline plot (stemtools::stem_inline()). Styling arguments (label size, palette, label options) tucked "Advanced options" disclosure.

title: STEM Chart block — new_stem_chart_block
code: new_stem_chart_block(   var = character(),   type = c("barplot", "inline"),   weight = character(),   label_size = 14,   palette = "div1",   direction = 1,   labels = TRUE,   label_accuracy = 1,   ... )

title: STEM Chart block — new_stem_chart_block
text: var Name variable (column) plot. empty, first column upstream data used. type Chart type, one "barplot" "inline". weight Optional name numeric column survey weights pass stemtools function. Empty (default) plots unweighted counts. label_size Size points inner data labels (percentage labels drawn bars). Defaults 14; use NA keep stemtools default sizing instead. restyle axis/legend text, use new_theme_stem_block() downstream instead. palette Name Stem palette passed plotting function, e.g. "div1". See stemtools::stem_palette(). direction Palette direction: 1 (default) -1 reverse. labels TRUE, print percentage label bar. label_accuracy Rounding accuracy labels; 1 gives whole numbers, 0.1 one decimal place. ... Forwarded blockr.ggplot::new_ggplot_transform_block().

title: STEM Chart block — new_stem_chart_block
text: ggplot transform block object class stem_chart_block.

title: STEM Chart block — new_stem_chart_block
code: new_stem_chart_block(var = "Species", type = "barplot", label_size = 5) #> <stem_chart_block<ggplot_transform_block<block>>> #> Name: "Stem chart" #> Data inputs: "data" #> Initial block state: #>  $ var           : chr "Species" #>  $ type          : chr "barplot" #>  $ weight        : chr(0) #>  $ label_size    : num 5 #>  $ palette       : chr "div1" #>  $ direction     : num 1 #>  $ labels        : logi TRUE #>  $ label_accuracy: num 1 #> Constructor: blockr.stem::new_stem_chart_block()

title: STEM Export block — new_stem_export_block
text: blockr block previews upstream ggplot lets user download PNG SVG image, native PowerPoint chart (.pptx), chosen size centimetres. Width defaults 14 cm; height defaults plot type - 6 cm inline plot (stemtools::stem_inline()) 10 cm bar plot (stemtools::stem_barplot()) - can overridden. block's output panel shows live preview plot exactly exported: produced ggplot2::ggsave() call download, chosen width/height (cm) set aspect ratio scaling factor changes apparent size text marks. preview updates automatically whenever format, width, height scaling inputs change.

title: STEM Export block — new_stem_export_block
code: new_stem_export_block(   format = c("png", "svg", "pptx"),   width = 14,   height = NA_real_,   scale = 1.5,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'stem_export_block' block_ui(id, x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'stem_export_block' block_output(x, result, session)

title: STEM Export block — new_stem_export_block
text: format Export format, one "png", "svg" "pptx". width Export width centimetres (default 14). height Export height centimetres. NA (default) picks height plot type (6 cm inline, 10 cm bar plot). scale Multiplicative scaling factor passed ggplot2::ggsave()'s scale argument PNG/SVG image formats (default 1.5). output size fixed, higher number makes plot's text marks appear smaller (lower number makes larger). Ignored PowerPoint chart export, rasterised. ... Forwarded blockr.ggplot::new_ggplot_transform_block(). id Passed blockr rendering block UI. x, result, session Passed blockr rendering block UI/output.

title: STEM Export block — new_stem_export_block
text: ggplot transform block object class stem_export_block.

title: STEM Export block — new_stem_export_block
text: SVG export uses ggplot2::ggsave()'s "svg" device, requires svglite package; installed SVG download reports error PNG can used instead. PowerPoint export writes native, editable Office chart (picture) via stem_write_pptx_chart() - recipient can Edit Data Excel restyle hand. requires mschart officer packages works STEM bar / inline plots (see stem_write_pptx_chart()).

title: STEM Export block — new_stem_export_block
code: new_stem_export_block(format = "png", width = 14) #> <stem_export_block<ggplot_transform_block<block>>> #> Name: "Stem export" #> Data inputs: "data" #> Initial block state: #>  $ format: chr "png" #>  $ width : num 14 #>  $ height: num NA #>  $ scale : num 1.5 #> Constructor: blockr.stem::new_stem_export_block()

title: STEM Import block — new_stem_import_block
text: blockr data block imports data set file - like "Import Data" block (read_block) blockr.io, file chosen graphical point--click file browser (via shinyFiles) instead typing path. root node analysis graph: takes data input provides data -stream blocks. stem_import_volumes() returns default set root directories offered file browser: user's home directory filesystem root.

title: STEM Import block — new_stem_import_block
code: new_stem_import_block(   file_path = character(),   args = list(),   volumes = stem_import_volumes(),   ... )  stem_import_volumes()

title: STEM Import block — new_stem_import_block
text: file_path Initial file path. Normally left empty set picking file browser. args Named list format-specific reader options (see Details). Normally left empty set via gear popover. volumes Named character vector root directories file browser may navigate, passed shinyFiles::shinyFileChoose(). Defaults stem_import_volumes() (Home filesystem root). ... Forwarded blockr.core::new_block().

title: STEM Import block — new_stem_import_block
text: data block object class stem_import_block.

title: STEM Import block — new_stem_import_block
text: selected file read reader matches format, mirroring read_block: delimited text (.csv/.tsv/.txt/...) readr::read_csv() friends, spreadsheets (.xlsx/.xls/...) readxl::read_excel(), everything else (.rds, .dta, .parquet, ...) rio::import(). SPSS files (.sav/.zsav/.por) read haven::read_spss() passed haven::as_factor(), labelled numeric columns arrive proper factors rather numeric codes. gear popover next file button exposes format-specific options (CSV delimiter/quote/encoding/skip/rows/header; Excel sheet/range/skip/rows/ header), shown matching file type - just like read_block. exists blockr.core's filebrowser_block builds output expression bare file path (bquote(.(file), ...)) rather call reads file, picking .rds file yields path string instead data. block wraps picked path real read call.

title: STEM Import block — new_stem_import_block
code: new_stem_import_block() #> <stem_import_block<file_block<block>>> #> Name: "Stem import" #> No data inputs #> Initial block state: #>  $ file_path: chr(0) #>  $ args     : list() #>  $ volumes  : Named chr [1:2] "/home/runner" "/" #>   ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:2] "Home" "Root" #> Constructor: blockr.stem::new_stem_import_block()

title: STEM Variable Selector block — new_stem_var_selector_block
text: blockr transform block lists categorical (factor / character) variables upstream data frame searchable table showing variable's name, label (attr(x, "label")) number categories. Clicking row selects variable; block outputs single selected column (label preserved), ready feed new_stem_chart_block() plotting - new_theme_stem_block() styling. block's output panel shows selected variable's (weighted) frequency distribution rather raw responses. block's value passed downstream blocks still underlying data, STEM Chart can plot .

title: STEM Variable Selector block — new_stem_var_selector_block
code: new_stem_var_selector_block(   var = character(),   weight = character(),   group = character(),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'stem_var_selector_block' block_output(x, result, session)

title: STEM Variable Selector block — new_stem_var_selector_block
text: var Name variable select. empty, first categorical variable used. Normally set clicking table row. weight Optional name numeric column survey weights carry forward. Empty (default) means unweighted. set, block outputs weight column alongside selected variable tags downstream new_stem_chart_block() / new_stem_visualize_block() weights plot automatically. group Optional name categorical column break plot . Empty (default) means grouping. set, downstream bar plot draws one stacked bar per category grouping variable (axis) shows selected variable coloured fill series - number colours selected variable's category count. Grouping applied stem_barplot(), stem_inline(). ... Forwarded blockr.core::new_transform_block(). x, result, session Passed blockr rendering block output.

title: STEM Variable Selector block — new_stem_var_selector_block
text: transform block object class stem_var_selector_block.

title: STEM Variable Selector block — new_stem_var_selector_block
text: table plot live separate blocks can moved around independently, chart's advanced settings downstream theme block stay fully accessible. Compose Selector -> new_stem_chart_block() -> new_theme_stem_block().

title: STEM Variable Selector block — new_stem_var_selector_block
code: new_stem_var_selector_block() #> <stem_var_selector_block<transform_block<block>>> #> Name: "Stem var selector" #> Data inputs: "data" #> Initial block state: #>  $ var   : chr(0) #>  $ weight: chr(0) #>  $ group : chr(0) #> Constructor: blockr.stem::new_stem_var_selector_block()

title: STEM Visualize block — new_stem_visualize_block
text: single blockr block plots variable stemtools function applies Stem theme, final, styled chart produced one place - combining everything new_stem_chart_block() new_theme_stem_block() offer without wire (jump ) two blocks. Chart options theme options live separate "Advanced" disclosures.

title: STEM Visualize block — new_stem_visualize_block
code: new_stem_visualize_block(   var = character(),   type = c("barplot", "inline"),   weight = character(),   label_size = 14,   palette = "div1",   direction = 1,   labels = TRUE,   label_accuracy = 1,   title_show = FALSE,   title_quote = FALSE,   title_wrap = 80,   ink = "black",   paper = "white",   accent = "#35978F",   family = "Calibri",   font_size = NA_real_,   padding = stem_default_padding,   ... )

title: STEM Visualize block — new_stem_visualize_block
text: var Name variable (column) plot. empty, first column upstream data used. type Chart type, one "barplot" "inline". weight Optional name numeric column survey weights. Empty (default) plots unweighted counts. label_size Size points inner data labels. Defaults 14; NA keeps stemtools default. palette, direction, labels, label_accuracy Chart styling passed stemtools function (see new_stem_chart_block()). title_show TRUE, draw plotted variable's label chart title. Defaults FALSE. title_quote TRUE, wrap title typographic quotation marks. takes effect title_show TRUE. Defaults FALSE. title_wrap Maximum number characters per title line; longer titles wrapped onto several lines overflow plot. takes effect title_show TRUE, affects ggplot-drawn title PNG/SVG (ggsave) exports - native PowerPoint chart unaffected. Defaults 80 (stemtools default). ink, paper, accent, family Stem theme arguments (see new_theme_stem_block() stemtools::theme_stem()). font_size Base text size points axis/legend/title text. NA (default) keeps theme's sizing. padding Padding millimetres added plot margin extreme axis labels (e.g. "0 %" / "100 %") clipped. Defaults 8 mm. ... Forwarded blockr.ggplot::new_ggplot_transform_block().

title: STEM Visualize block — new_stem_visualize_block
text: ggplot transform block object class stem_visualize_block.

title: STEM Visualize block — new_stem_visualize_block
code: new_stem_visualize_block(var = "Species", accent = "#35978F") #> <stem_visualize_block<ggplot_transform_block<block>>> #> Name: "Stem visualize" #> Data inputs: "data" #> Initial block state: #>  $ var           : chr "Species" #>  $ type          : chr "barplot" #>  $ weight        : chr(0) #>  $ label_size    : num 14 #>  $ palette       : chr "div1" #>  $ direction     : num 1 #>  $ labels        : logi TRUE #>  $ label_accuracy: num 1 #>  $ title_show    : logi FALSE #>  $ title_quote   : logi FALSE #>  $ title_wrap    : num 80 #>  $ ink           : chr "black" #>  $ paper         : chr "white" #>  $ accent        : chr "#35978F" #>  $ family        : chr "Calibri" #>  $ font_size     : num NA #>  $ padding       : num 8 #> Constructor: blockr.stem::new_stem_visualize_block()

title: Theme STEM block — new_theme_stem_block
text: blockr transform block applies complete Stem ggplot2 theme (stemtools::theme_stem()) upstream ggplot2 plot. works like blockr.ggplot Theme block, instead exposing individual theme elements applies Stem house theme, exposing theme's arguments (ink, paper, accent, family) block controls.

title: Theme STEM block — new_theme_stem_block
code: new_theme_stem_block(   ink = "black",   paper = "white",   accent = "#35978F",   family = "Calibri",   font_size = NA_real_,   padding = stem_default_padding,   ... )

title: Theme STEM block — new_theme_stem_block
text: ink Foreground colour used text, titles axis lines. paper Background colour behind panel, plot, strips legend keys. accent Accent colour (e.g. fill ggplot2::geom_smooth()). Defaults primary Stem brand colour. family Font family text. Defaults "Calibri", Stem house font. blank string uses graphics device's default family. font_size Base text size points axis, legend title text. Use NA (default) keep theme's sizing. controls plot's structural text; resize inner data labels new_stem_chart_block(), use block's label-size control. padding Padding millimetres added plot margin extreme axis labels (e.g. "0 %" / "100 %") clipped. Defaults 8 mm. ... Forwarded blockr.ggplot::new_ggplot_transform_block().

title: Theme STEM block — new_theme_stem_block
text: ggplot transform block object class theme_stem_block.

title: Theme STEM block — new_theme_stem_block
code: new_theme_stem_block(accent = "#35978F", font_size = 14) #> <theme_stem_block<ggplot_transform_block<block>>> #> Name: "Theme stem" #> Data inputs: "data" #> Initial block state: #>  $ ink      : chr "black" #>  $ paper    : chr "white" #>  $ accent   : chr "#35978F" #>  $ family   : chr "Calibri" #>  $ font_size: num 14 #>  $ padding  : num 8 #> Constructor: blockr.stem::new_theme_stem_block()  if (interactive()) {   library(blockr.core)   # The block needs a ggplot input, see app.R for a full board demo.   serve(new_theme_stem_block()) }

what: Resize a plot's data labels — set_label_size
title: Resize a plot's data labels — set_label_size
text: Adjusts size text drawn ggplot2::geom_text() ggplot2::geom_label() layers plot - e.g. percentage labels inside stemtools::stem_barplot() stemtools::stem_inline() - leaving axis, legend title text untouched. Used new_stem_chart_block() expose inner-label size control.

title: Resize a plot's data labels — set_label_size
Resize a plot's data labels — set_label_size
text: plot ggplot object. size Label size points.

title: Resize a plot's data labels — set_label_size
text: plot, size text/label layers updated.

title: Resize a plot's data labels — set_label_size

what: Export a STEM plot as a native, editable PowerPoint chart — stem_write_pptx_chart
title: Export a STEM plot as a native, editable PowerPoint chart — stem_write_pptx_chart
text: Writes .pptx file containing native Microsoft Office chart - real c:chart object backed embedded Excel worksheet - rather picture plot. recipient can open deck use Chart Design > Edit Data change numbers hand Excel, restyle chart, etc.

title: Export a STEM plot as a native, editable PowerPoint chart — stem_write_pptx_chart
code: stem_write_pptx_chart(plot, file, width = NA_real_, height = NA_real_)

title: Export a STEM plot as a native, editable PowerPoint chart — stem_write_pptx_chart
text: plot ggplot object produced STEM chart / visualize block. file Path .pptx file write. width, height Size chart slide, centimetres. Default NA fills slide (leaving small margin); pass explicit values place fixed-size, centred chart instead.

title: Export a STEM plot as a native, editable PowerPoint chart — stem_write_pptx_chart
text: file, invisibly.

title: Export a STEM plot as a native, editable PowerPoint chart — stem_write_pptx_chart
text: native Office chart built data (pixels), works STEM bar / inline plots, whose summarised data aesthetic mapping recovered ggplot object (stemtools::stem_barplot(), stemtools::stem_inline()). category axis, (optional) coloured series values read back plot; Stem palette colours carried chart's series looks right box. ggplot raises error - export PNG SVG instead. chart inherits plot's Stem theme (font family ink colour stemtools::theme_stem(), legend top) matches -screen plot. native chart can freely resized PowerPoint, placed filling slide default rather fixed centimetre size. Requires mschart officer packages.

title: blockr.stem 0.1.3
text: STEM Visualize — new chart title options “Chart options”, / stemtools defaults existing charts unchanged. Requires stemtools (>= 0.1.2). Show title (title_show) draws plotted variable’s label chart title (rendered bold left-aligned theme_stem()). Add title quotes (title_quote) wraps title typographic quotation marks. Title wrap (title_wrap) sets maximum characters per title line long titles wrap instead overflowing. Affects PNG/SVG (ggsave) exports ; native PowerPoint chart unaffected. STEM Export — title now carries every export format. PNG/SVG render automatically; native PowerPoint chart reconstructs title (including bold face) left-aligns match theme_stem(), editing chart OOXML written .pptx. STEM Import — SPSS files (.sav/.zsav/.por) now read haven::read_spss() wrapped haven::as_factor(), labelled numeric columns arrive proper factors (value labels) rather bare numeric codes rio::import() left behind — downstream survey blocks expect.

title: blockr.stem 0.1.1
text: STEM Export — PowerPoint chart preview now renders text size exported native chart. preview scaled match chart’s data labels (1 / stem_pptx_label_scale), longer shows oversized fonts relative downloaded .pptx. STEM Export — fixed category (y-axis) order exported PowerPoint chart, came reversed relative preview PNG/SVG exports. reversal accounts mschart ordering category axis row order single-series bar charts factor levels grouped/stacked charts.

title: blockr.stem 0.1.0
text: First release. STEM Import (new_stem_import_block()) — root data block imports data set file chosen point--click file browser (shinyFiles). Delimited text read readr, spreadsheets readxl, everything else (.rds, .sav, .dta, .parquet, …) rio::import(). Format-specific options exposed via gear popover. STEM Variable Selector (new_stem_var_selector_block()) — searchable table categorical variables (name, label, category count); click row output single column plotting. Optional survey-weight grouping selections carried downstream plot blocks. output panel shows selected variable’s (weighted) frequency distribution. STEM Chart (new_stem_chart_block()) — plots single variable stemtools chart: bar plot (stem_barplot()) inline plot (stem_inline()), palette, label, weight grouping options. STEM Visualize (new_stem_visualize_block()) — plots variable applies Stem theme one block, combining every STEM Chart Theme STEM setting produce final, styled chart. Theme STEM (new_theme_stem_block()) — applies Stem ggplot2 theme (stemtools::theme_stem()) upstream plot, controlling ink, paper, accent font family. STEM Export (new_stem_export_block()) — previews upstream plot downloads PNG SVG chosen size centimetres, native, editable PowerPoint chart (.pptx) via stem_write_pptx_chart().
